source.title: Neural ensembles in CA3 transiently encode paths forward of the animal at a decision point

confidence_rationale: High source quality (Journal of Neuroscience, in vivo rodent tetrodes, well-replicated finding). Strong mapping fidelity for ARC-018 prospective rollout: CA3 ensembles sweep forward through possible trajectories at choice points, which is the neural implementation of ARC-018's counterfactual rollout function. Mapping fidelity for MECH-033 is moderate: the data show trajectory-forward sweeps in CA3 but do not characterise E2-equivalent action-consequence structures. Transfer risk moderate: spatial navigation task, rodent CA3.
failure_signatures: CA3 neural ensembles transiently encode positions ahead of the animal at decision points — the encoded position sweeps down each arm of the maze before commitment.
Prospective encoding is transient and occurs specifically at choice points, not during continuous running.

mapping.source_claim_statement: At T-maze decision points, CA3 neural ensemble activity transiently represents positions ahead of the animal's current location, sweeping forward down each possible arm of the maze in a pattern consistent with vicarious trial and error (VTE) behavior. The forward sweeps precede commitment and represent future trajectories rather than past or current locations.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-018 claims hippocampus performs counterfactual trajectory construction at commitment boundaries. Johnson & Redish provide the canonical cellular evidence for this: CA3 sweeps through prospective trajectory representations at exactly the moment when E3 is deciding (the commitment boundary). The theta-compressed forward sweep is the neural implementation of the rollout that ARC-018 posits. For MECH-033, the E2 kernel → hippocampal rollout handoff is evidenced here at the level of CA3 dynamics: the trajectory chaining happens in CA3 during theta cycles, constrained by the available action paths. The CA3 autoassociative circuit chains trajectory states forward during theta, seeded by E2-equivalent transition kernels from entorhinal cortex.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper shows spatial trajectory sweeps in a simple T-maze. ARC-018 requires generalisation to abstract z_world action-consequence space — the spatial→abstract mapping is inferential. MECH-033 requires the kernel-seeding mechanism to specifically use E2 action-consequence objects; the paper shows trajectory chaining in CA3 but does not characterise the input structure (EC inputs = E2 kernels is a theoretical mapping, not directly observed). Rodent data; primate/human generalization supported by separate literature.
mapping.source_context: Rodent CA3 tetrode recordings during T-maze navigation with reward at both arms. Bayesian position reconstruction from ensemble firing reveals prospective position encoding during VTE pauses at choice points.

confidence_components.notes: Strong cellular evidence from CA3 tetrodes. Mapping fidelity good for ARC-018 (trajectory lookahead at commitment boundary) but moderate for MECH-033 (requires E2 action-consequence kernel interpretation of EC inputs). Transfer risk: spatial maze to abstract z_world.
